name: Artifact Intake and Claim Table Prototype
controlling_principle: CyberShield does not ask whether we trust a person. It asks which claims, artifacts, evidence paths, and reliance decisions can be trusted, at what confidence, for what purpose, and with what risk if wrong.
boundary: Static advisory prototype only. The artifact intake and claim table do not ingest live URLs, retrieve internet content, extract claims automatically, verify evidence, calculate live scores, or perform political validation.
purpose: Create a static artifact intake panel and editable-looking claim table so users can understand the future Internet Trust workflow without live ingestion or live verification claims.

artifact_intake.artifact_title: USAFacts / Steve Ballmer government responsibilities video
artifact_intake.reliance_purpose: Executive education, civic literacy, decision support, public briefing preparation, and artifact trust demonstration
artifact_intake.audience: executives, analysts, educators, board members, public-sector leaders
artifact_intake.risk_if_wrong: Bad briefing, incorrect public explanation, flawed policy interpretation, reputational damage, or inappropriate reliance on outdated fiscal assumptions

claim_rows.sentence: Federal, state, and local governments divide responsibility for public services in different ways.
claim_rows.missing_evidence: Official source definitions and scope notes
claim_rows.next_step: Verify against official government data definitions before executive briefing reliance.

claim_rows.sentence: Medicaid funding involves both federal and state government responsibility.
claim_rows.reliance_risk: high if used for policy or financial decision
claim_rows.missing_evidence: Current Medicaid matching rules, state-specific context, and fiscal year reference
claim_rows.next_step: Verify current Medicaid matching rules and state-specific context before reliance.

claim_rows.sentence: Education funding responsibilities differ across federal, state, and local levels.
claim_rows.reliance_risk: moderate to high depending on jurisdiction and year
claim_rows.confidence_label: prototype-low until jurisdiction and year are confirmed
claim_rows.missing_evidence: Funding split by year, jurisdiction, and source methodology
claim_rows.next_step: Verify funding splits by year, jurisdiction, and source methodology.

claim_rows.sentence: A data-driven artifact can still require claim-level verification before leadership relies on it.
claim_rows.missing_evidence: None for doctrine demonstration; real artifact use still requires verification
claim_rows.next_step: Use as CyberShield doctrine example, not as verified external fact output.

editable_prototype_behavior: Fields appear editable to show future workflow intent
Changes are local UI state only and are not saved to a backend
Claim rows can be inspected but are not produced by live extraction
Proof Pack readiness labels are static advisory placeholders
All reliance must remain bounded by prototype limitations
definition_of_done: Static artifact intake appears in existing workspaces only
Editable-looking claim table appears in existing workspaces only
No live ingestion or extraction claims
Claim rows map to evidence states and Trust Scoring Models
Claim table can export text summary
